info.dbsa: blisters, erosions, Nikolsky+, necrotic — erythema excluded (grey).

login.subtitle: Clinical study tool
login.error: Invalid email or password. Please try again.
login.lockedOut: Too many failed attempts. Please wait {seconds}s before trying again.
login.footer: Contact the study administrator if you need an account.

forgotPassword.title: Forgot Password
forgotPassword.subtitle: Enter your email to receive a password reset link.
forgotPassword.success: If an account exists with that email, you will receive a password reset link shortly. Please check your inbox.
forgotPassword.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.

resetPassword.title: Reset Password
resetPassword.tooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.
resetPassword.success: Your password has been reset successfully. Redirecting to login…
resetPassword.error: Failed to reset password. The link may have expired. Please try again.

changePassword.title: Change Password
changePassword.tooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.
changePassword.error: Failed to change password. Check your current password and try again.

admin.title: Clinician Management
admin.name: Full Name
admin.resetPasswordHelp: Minimum 8 characters. The clinician will use this to log in.

dialog.error: Failed to send invite. Please try again.

patients.title: Patients
patients.noPatients: No patients registered yet. Add a patient to begin.

assessment.title: Assessment
assessment.albuminHelp: Serum albumin level at time of assessment.
assessment.photosHelp: Optional. Tap to take a photo or select from gallery.
assessment.submitError: Failed to submit assessment. Please try again.
assessment.confirmMessage: This will be saved to the patient record and cannot be edited.
assessment.photoEditorHelp: Draw black areas to anonymise faces. Tap a photo thumbnail to edit.
assessment.loadPreviousMessage: Load the body map from the previous assessment ({date}) as a starting point? You can then add or erase areas.

patientDetail.noAssessments: No assessments yet. Start a new assessment to begin.

connection.offline: No internet connection — please reconnect before submitting.

mfa.verifySubtitle: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.
mfa.lockedOut: Too many failed attempts. Please wait {seconds}s before trying again.
mfa.enrollSubtitle: Scan this QR code with your authenticator app (e.g. Google Authenticator, Authy).
mfa.enrollRequired: Two-factor authentication is required to use this application.
mfa.resetConfirm: Reset this clinician's MFA? They will need to set it up again on next login.

local.subtitle: This is local mode — data is saved on this device only. Sign in to use the full clinical tool.
local.saveError: Could not save file. Please try again.
local.loadNoData: No saved data found for this file.

sites.title: Study Sites
sites.keyHelp: Lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only (e.g. germany, usa-east).

overview.title: Patient Overview
overview.noPatients: No patients in the study yet.

export.title: Export Assessment Data
export.description: Download all assessment data as a CSV file for analysis.

setup.title: Start a New Trial
setup.subtitle: Follow this guide to deploy your own instance of the SJS/TEN BSA Assessment Tool.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: This tool lets clinicians draw on body maps to assess skin involvement in SJS/TEN, capture photographs, calculate SCORTEN severity scores, and track patients across multiple assessments. Each trial needs its own deployed instance with its own Supabase database.
overview.vercel: A Vercel account (for deployment — free tier works)
overview.deepl: A DeepL API key (free tier — for note translation)

forkClone.title: Fork & Clone the Repository
forkClone.description: Fork the repository to your own GitHub account, then clone it locally.
forkClone.step1: Click the button below to fork the repository on GitHub:
forkClone.step2: Then clone your fork and install dependencies:

supabase.title: Create a Supabase Project
supabase.description: Supabase provides the PostgreSQL database, authentication, and file storage.
supabase.step1: Go to the Supabase dashboard and create a new project:
supabase.step2: Choose a project name (e.g. "my-ten-study") and set a strong database password.
supabase.step3Region: Select a region close to your study sites. For EU data, choose Frankfurt (eu-central-1).
supabase.step4Keys: Once created, go to Settings > API and note down:
supabase.keyAnonDesc: safe to use in browser code
supabase.keyServiceDesc: server-side only — never expose in client code

migrations.title: Run Database Migrations
migrations.description: Run these SQL scripts in your Supabase SQL Editor, in order. Each script builds on the previous one.
migrations.howTo: Go to SQL Editor in your Supabase dashboard, paste each script, and click Run.
migrations.desc1: Core tables, enums, RLS policies, audit triggers, indexes, and canvas storage bucket.
migrations.desc2: Adds albumin level column and assessment photos table with RLS policies.
migrations.desc3: Adds notes language and translation columns for bilingual note support.
migrations.desc4: Adds EXIF metadata column to photos table.
migrations.desc5: Adds SCORTEN severity index columns (7 criteria + score) to assessments.

sites.title: Customise Your Study Sites
sites.description: The default database schema uses "france" and "england" as study sites. Replace these with your own sites before running migration 001.
sites.genSqlTitle: 1. Modify the CREATE TYPE line in migration 001:
sites.genTypeTitle: 2. Update the TypeScript type in src/lib/supabase/types.ts (line 10):
sites.genUiTitle: 3. Update the site dropdowns in these 3 files:
sites.genI18nTitle: 4. Update the site names in messages/en.json and messages/fr.json under admin.sites:
sites.genDefaultTitle: 5. Update the default site in src/app/page.tsx (line 42) and src/app/admin/components/ClinicianManagement.tsx (line 44):
sites.validation: Site keys must be lowercase letters only (a-z), no spaces or numbers.
sites.alreadyRan: If you already ran migration 001 with the default sites, run this ALTER TYPE instead:

adminUser.title: Create Your First Admin User
adminUser.description: Create the initial PI (Principal Investigator) account. This is the bootstrap account that can then invite all other clinicians from within the app.
adminUser.passwordHelp: Minimum 8 characters. You'll use this to log in for the first time.
adminUser.siteHelp: Must match a study site key (e.g. france, england).
adminUser.success: PI account created! You can now log in at the login page.
adminUser.note: This can only be used once. After the first admin is created, use the invite system from within the app to add more users.

envConfig.title: Configure Environment Variables
envConfig.description: Create a .env.local file in your project root with your Supabase and DeepL credentials.
envConfig.urlNote: Found in Supabase dashboard > Settings > API > Project URL
envConfig.anonNote: Found in Settings > API > anon public
envConfig.serviceNote: Found in Settings > API > service_role (keep secret!)
envConfig.deeplNote: Get a free key at deepl.com/pro-api — ends with :fx for free tier
envConfig.deeplOptional: The DeepL key is optional. Without it, note translation will be skipped.

deploy.title: Deploy Your Instance
deploy.localUrl: Open http://localhost:3000 and log in with your admin account from step 6.
deploy.vercelDesc: For production use, deploy to Vercel:
deploy.vercelStep1: Push your code to GitHub (with your site customisations from step 5).
deploy.vercelStep3: Set the same 4 environment variables in Vercel project settings (Settings > Environment Variables).
deploy.vercelStep4: Deploy. Vercel auto-deploys on each push to main.
deploy.vercelAlt: Or deploy from the command line:
deploy.pwaNoteDesc: Once deployed, the app can be installed as a home screen app on iPad/iPhone via Safari's Share > Add to Home Screen.

firstLogin.title: First Login & Next Steps
firstLogin.description: Your instance is ready. Here's what happens on first login:
firstLogin.mfaDesc: On first login, you'll be prompted to set up MFA using an authenticator app (Google Authenticator, Authy, etc.). This is required for all users.
firstLogin.addCliniciansDesc: Go to the Admin dashboard to add clinician accounts. Each clinician will set up their own MFA on first login.
firstLogin.roleClinician: Clinician — can create patients and submit assessments
firstLogin.roleAdmin: Admin — can manage clinicians, view audit log, export data
firstLogin.startDesc: Create your first patient from the home page, then tap New Assessment to begin drawing on the body maps.
firstLogin.congratulations: Your SJS/TEN BSA Assessment Tool is ready to use!
